question: What's the best time to visit Kalbarri WA with kids?
answer: April to October is absolutely perfect for families! You'll get mild, sunny days ideal for beach play and exploring the gorges. Winter (June-August) is beautifully warm compared to down south, averaging 20-25°C. Avoid the stinking hot summer months (Dec-Feb) when temperatures can hit 40°C - too rough for little ones. School holidays in April, July and September are popular, so book your holiday park early!

question: Are there pet-friendly holiday parks in Kalbarri WA?
answer: Yes, mate! Several Kalbarri holiday parks welcome your furry family members. Most pet-friendly parks require dogs to be leashed and kept in designated areas. You'll typically need to bring vaccination certificates and pay a small pet fee (around $10-15 per night). Always ring ahead to confirm their pet policy and book a pet-friendly cabin or site, as they're limited and book out fast!

question: How much do holiday parks cost in Kalbarri WA during school holidays?
answer: Expect to pay $150-$300 per night for cabins during school holidays, while powered sites run $45-$80. Prices peak during Easter and September/October school breaks. Book 3-6 months ahead for better rates and availability. Off-peak periods (Feb-March, Nov) can be 30-40% cheaper. Many parks offer weekly discounts too - great if you can stretch your stay to a full week!

question: What should I pack for a holiday park stay with young kids?
answer: Bring your own linen (sheets, towels, pillows) as most parks don't include these. Pack plenty of sunscreen, hats, swimmers, and rash vests. Don't forget bikes, beach toys, fishing gear, and outdoor games. Essentials include insect repellent, first aid kit, basic cooking supplies, and food - Kalbarri's shops are limited. Chuck in books and indoor games for quiet time too!

question: What is there to do near Kalbarri WA holiday parks for families?
answer: Heaps! The coastal cliffs and Nature's Window are must-sees (easy walks for little legs). Kids love Blue Holes beach, pelican feeding at 8:45am daily, and the skywalk lookouts. Rainbow Jungle bird park is brilliant for toddlers. River canoeing, rock pool exploring, and beach swimming fill your days perfectly. Most holiday parks have playgrounds, pools and jumping pillows on-site too!
